authors: Stauss T, El Majdoub F, Sayed D, Surges G, Rosenberg WS, Kapural L, Bundschu R, Lalkhen A, Patel N, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Rotte A, Edgar DR, Bettag M, Maarouf M
title: A multicenter real-world review of 10 kHz SCS outcomes for treatment of chronic trunk and/or limb pain
journal_name: Annals of Clinical and Translational Neurology
study_question: Is high-frequency spinal cord stimulation at 10 kHz effective for chronic trunk and limb pain in real-world settings?
population_assessed: 1660 patients with chronic trunk and/or limb pain trialed or implanted with 10 kHz HF-SCS between April 2014 and January 2018.
follow_up_duration: 3, 6, 12 months post-implantation; mean 8.9 months to last visit
outcome_measures: Responder rate (≥50% pain relief), pain intensity, function, sleep, quality of life, medication intake
inclusion_criteria: Patients with chronic trunk and/or limb pain trialed or permanently implanted with HF-SCS at 10 kHz between April 2014 and January 2018.
answer_to_study_question: Over 70% of HF-SCS-treated patients experienced sustained pain relief throughout 12 months, with improvements in function, sleep, and quality of life.
